summary: The plan is close, but it has one blocking wiring error and one scope gap that would leave some unit displays stuck on imperial.

issues.section: Step 6: Wire provider into layouts / File Map
issues.title: Target layout file for `(main)` does not exist
issues.description: The plan specifies `src/app/(main)/layout.tsx`, but this codebase has no such file. Existing `(main)` layouts are `src/app/(main)/kit/layout.tsx`, `src/app/(main)/products/layout.tsx`, and `src/app/(main)/u/layout.tsx`. As written, this step cannot be implemented and `(main)` routes would miss provider wiring.
issues.suggestion: Revise Step 6 to wire the provider at an actual shared ancestor (e.g. `src/app/layout.tsx` or `AuthAwareShell`) or explicitly wire all existing `(main)` sub-layouts.

issues.title: Planned provider scope may exclude `ProductDetailPanel` consumers
issues.description: Step 6 says to "wrap children with provider". In current structure, `ProductDetailPanel` is rendered inside `AppShell`, not inside the `children` slot. Step 7 updates `product-detail-content.tsx` to read `useUnitPreference()`, and that component is used by `ProductDetailPanel`. If only `children` are wrapped, panel renders outside the provider and falls back to imperial.
issues.suggestion: Place `UnitPreferenceProvider` above `AppShell` (or inside `AppShell`/`AuthAwareShell` at the top) so both routed content and shell-owned components like `ProductDetailPanel` share the same context.
